title: Aulomyrcia subcordata
intro: Aulomyrcia subcordata, also known as the Guiana mucilaj, is a plant species belonging to the Myrtaceae family, native to the Guiana Shield region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering relief from various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Aulomyrcia subcordata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Aulomyrcia subcordata is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including coughs, colds, and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Aulomyrcia subcordata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. This family includes a wide range of plants commonly known as myrtles or eucalyptus. The Myrtaceae family is part of the order Myrtales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The order Myrtales is further grouped into the clade Rosidae, a large clade of flowering plants that also includes the families Rosaceae and Fabaceae. Aulomyrcia subcordata is a member of the clade Rosidae, which is a part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as angiosperms.
